title: Hattie's Giant Robot and the Rainbow Gnome Hat
summary: Hattie the mouse is a master builder who thrives on creative projects. After surprising her friends Mickey, Minnie, and Mole with a massive, building-sized metal robot in a forest clearing, she moves on to her next creation: a multi-colored cardboard hat designed for all three friends to share. To perfect her masterpiece, Hattie travels to the Mousey Grocery Store to find the ideal purple gnome sticker. Her journey showcases her ingenuity as she balances engineering giant machines with the delicate art of hat-making.

highlightQuote.text: Pink, and yellow, and blue, and red, and gold, and orange, and yellow, and orange, and yellow, and orange, and yellow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now once upon a time, Mickey and Minnie and Mole were on an afternoon walk together through the forest when they heard all of a sudden the strangest sound. It was like bang-bang-bang-bang-bang. And they thought, 'I wonder what that banging sound is?' And Mole said, 'Sounds like someone hammering.' And Mickey said, 'Yeah, but hammering on what?' And Minnie said, 'I think it's coming from over that way.' And so they cut through the trees and they got to a clearing, which is a big empty space with no trees in it. And there they saw two gigantic metal feet. Each foot was bigger than Mickey or Minnie. And they looked up above the feet and they saw legs, and above the legs was a body and arms and they looked straight up high, high up in the sky and there was a big head. And perched on the shoulder of this giant metal creature was Hattie the Mouse. And she had a little hammer and she was going bang-bang-bang-bang-bang. And Mickey Mouse said, 'Oh my gosh, Hattie, what are you doing up there?' And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie said, 'I'm working on my robot. Do you like it?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mole said, 'Wow, that is a big robot.' And Hattie said, 'It's almost done. Would you like to go for a ride?' And what did Mickey and Minnie and Mole say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nope.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you don't want to go for... well, you can stay down there and I'll show you what this robot can do, said Hattie Mouse. And so Hattie the Mouse crawled into a little chair which was sitting on the shoulder of the robot and strapped herself in and pressed a button and [robot noises] the robot came to life. And its arms swung up and its eyes opened [ping ping] and it started to lift one leg at a time [crunch] and when its feet stepped on the ground they made a huge crunching sound because the robot was as big as a building, it was as big as a house. And Hattie said, 'Wow, it works!' And Mickey and Minnie and Mole said, 'Hattie, why did you build a giant robot?' And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Well, it's just a fun project. I wondered if I could make a giant robot, so I tried, and look, I did it!' And what could they do with their giant robot now that they had it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. I think we could do another story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, do you want to do a different one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, the beginning of the Hattie's workshop making a hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And once upon a time, Hattie was at her workshop making a hat. And what kind of hat was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a Mickey and Minnie and Mole hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was a hat for all three of them? Yeah, they share it. That's a nice idea. And what did it look like, this hat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pink, and yellow, and blue, and red, and gold, and orange, and yellow, and orange, and yellow, and orange, and yellow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. And Mole's part was gray, so it was also gray too. Oh, I see. And what was it made of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Metal. No, it was made of cardboard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah. And did it have decorations on it? What was on it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stickers and decorations and they were gnome decorations.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And was it almost done?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'But wait, there's one more thing I need to add.' What was the one more thing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Decorations. One special decoration. Gnome decoration.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Gnome decoration, is that what you said? Oh, a gnome decoration. 'It needs a little gnome, but I don't have one,' said Hattie to herself. 'I wonder how I can get a gnome to decorate this hat...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, you could get a sticker gnome.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a sticker gnome. 'But I don't have a sticker gnome either. Where can I get one?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: At a mousey grocery store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the mousey grocery store. Good idea. And so Hattie left her workshop and walked down the road where there were a few stores all together. There was a little cafe, and a bookstore, and a mousey grocery store. And so Hattie went into the mousey grocery store and shopped around. And what did she see in there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Big gnome stickers, small gnome stickers, neon gnome stickers, teeny gnome stickers, big gnome stickers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They had everything. Paper gnome stickers, cardboard gnome stickers, wow, and plastic ones... and plastic... gnome stickers. Which kind did she choose?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know which kind she chose.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said to herself, 'Hmm, I better pick a gnome sticker that will fit perfectly on the hat. Maybe I'll pick this medium-sized gnome sticker. Now, should I get the purple one or the blue one?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The purple one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So she picked the purple gnome sticker that was medium-sized so it would be just right for the hat. And she paid the mouse who owned the mousey grocery store for the sticker and then she thought to herself, 'Great, this is going to be an excellent hat.' And so she went back to her workshop and she peeled the sticker and then she stuck it right on the hat. And then the hat was done. And what'd she do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. Maybe we could just turn off the stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, are we all done with stars and stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A resourceful, perpetually humming mole with an impossibly long name, this polite and intrepid mariner serves as a bridge between worlds, translating Frasgle’s unique tongue and guiding the way to the hidden Underground Library. Whether managing rocket controls with wide-eyed curiosity, unraveling treehouse mysteries, or sharing the magic of special Legos, he encourages his companions to embrace the unknown between tending his dandelion yard and baking fragrant banana bread. A lover of both wriggly worms and sweet blueberries, he sings whimsical songs of carnabee sharks and drives a noisy dark brown car, remaining firmly hesitant about boarding giant robots even as he coaxes his friends toward bold new adventures.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Giant Robot
characters.description: A building-sized metal creation built by Hattie as a fun project.

characters.name: Sticker Gnome
characters.description: A medium-sized purple decoration added to Hattie's latest hat.

places.name: The Forest
places.description: Cloaking the surface of the green planet in a vast mantle of dense greenery, this whispering woodland serves as the lush setting of the 'Forest Friends' chronicle. It is a realm where creatures play eternal hide-and-seek between Hattie’s cozy clinic and the ancestral cradle of Finn’s companions. Along magical trails where Hattie and the Pakruder Worm harvest hat-making ingredients, winding paths veil secret gopher tunnels and hidden mole cottages, forming the essential route toward the wizard's castle. While its sun-drenched threshold remains a sanctuary for the Rainbow Giraffe and Mama Bird, the deeper, darker thickets harbor a somber mystery; it was within these sprawling, uncharted depths—the same eerie expanse where the sick creature was first spotted and Mossy was discovered—that Mole recently vanished into the shadows.

places.name: Clearing
places.description: A big empty space in the forest where Hattie keeps her robot.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: Mousey Grocery Store
places.description: A shop that sells a vast array of gnome-themed stickers.

places.name: Cafe
places.description: A small shop located near the mousey grocery store.

places.name: Bookstore
places.description: A local shop situated on the same road as the workshop and grocery store.
